published_prior_art.cite: AtheyImbens2006 + CallawayLi2019: two-period/non-staggered CIC and copula-stability QTT identification baseline
LiLin2024: staggered QTT under group-invariance copula and flags challenges for Callaway-Li time-invariance extension, but no CIC-CL iff frontier
LiLin2024 + MillerGalvao2025: close staggered-QTT comparators, but no kappa/kappa-star sign-flip threshold
GoodmanBacon2021/SunAbraham2021/deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020 mean decompositions plus MillerGalvao2025 staggered-QTT estimation; no exact CIC-CL correction
AtheyImbens2006 + CallawayLi2019: standard two-period QTT identification corner

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: Kim--Wooldridge 2025 JBES and Miller--Galvao 2026 SSRN are named in Section 8 but have no bibliography entries.

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: ChernozhukovFernandezValLuo2024 is unstable/incorrect: arXiv:2409.02311 resolves to a different Distribution Regression DID paper with different authors.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The non-staggered timing-homogeneity baseline is not a new flagship result relative to AtheyImbens2006 and CallawayLi2019.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist omits closer staggered/quantile DID comparators KimWooldridge2025 and MillerGalvao2025; tier=not-publishable below novelty_target=flagship until these are integrated.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: GoodmanBacon2021 and SunAbraham2021 are used as flagship decomposition comparators without section/equation/page anchors.

novelty_flags.label: Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: ChernozhukovFernandezValLuo2024 is mischaracterized: the cited arXiv id does not match the stated title/authors.

soundness_flags.label: Setup / kappa
soundness_flags.one_line: mu_G is defined on treated cohorts plus infinity, but kappa, bar-g, and Var_mu_G(g) take means/covariances over g including infinity.

soundness_flags.label: Setup / CL functional
soundness_flags.one_line: The CL counterfactual CDF uses an undefined copula inverse and an integral with d-dot; the treated-cohort dependence equality needed for CL recovery is not stated.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption a:cohort requires full support including never-treated, while Theorem 1 assumes mu_G is degenerate at one treated cohort.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 proof sketch
soundness_flags.one_line: Rank invariance does not generally make the copula of untreated change and baseline comonotone; h(U,t)-h(U,g-1) need not be increasing in U.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary 2 (concrete witness)
soundness_flags.one_line: The Gaussian location-scale untreated path with common h(U,t) makes CIC recover the untreated counterfactual; with the stated location-shift effects the claimed nonzero opposite-sign cohort gaps are not obtained.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: pi_g(mu_G;tau) is not defined, so the negative-weight claim, sum-g w_g algebra, and corrected aggregate theta-star are not mathematically checkable.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine mu_G as a distribution over treated cohorts only and keep the never-treated group as a separate comparison population.
Replace the CL counterfactual display with a precise Callaway-Li recovery functional and state the exact copula-stability/dependence equality for treated cohorts.
Recompute the Gaussian witness; if it collapses to delta=0, replace it with a verified finite-support or parametric witness where CIC and CL truly diverge.
Add bibliography and location anchors for Kim-Wooldridge 2025 and Miller-Galvao 2025/2026, then state exactly why kappa/kappa-star is not subsumed by their staggered-QTT estimators.

recommended_next_step: Reject this draft and redraft the mathematical kernel around a well-defined treated-cohort timing law, a formal CL functional, and a verified nonzero witness.
